ORDER REGARDING DEFENDANT WEST PORTAL ORAL AND FACIAL SURGERY CENTERS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION

DEFENDANT WEST PORTAL ORAL AND FACIAL SURGERY CENTER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED, AND THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. (CODE CIV. PROC., 1281.4.)BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION, THERE IS NO DISPUTE THAT THE PARTIES ARE SUBJECT TO A BINDING ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THE COURT ENCOURAGES THE PARTIES TO MEDIATE THEIR DISPUTE, BUT IT LACKS THE AUTHORITY TO GRANT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO COMPEL MEDIATION. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WEST PORTAL ORAL AND FACIAL SURGERY CENTER'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendant West Portal Oral and Facial Surgery Center's motion to compel arbitration is granted, and the action is stayed pending the completion of arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc., 1281.4.)

Based on plaintiff's opposition, there is no dispute that the parties are subject to a binding arbitration agreement. The court encourages the parties to mediate their dispute, but it lacks the authority to grant plaintiff's request to compel mediation.

"Essential to the mediation process is the concept that the parties are in control of resolving their own dispute." (Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, 540.) Mediation is voluntary. (Jen-Weld, 146 Cal.App.4th at 541.) To that end, the court cannot force parties to mediate because an order compelling mediation is antithetical to the entire concept of mediation. (Id. at p. 543.) The court notes that it will enforce contractual sanctions where the parties have agreed to such a sanction for failing to attempt to mediate. (See Cullen v. Corwin (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1074, 1079 [provision barring recovery of attorney's fees because party failed to mediate enforced].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)
